Miriam Davidson-Simply Together

Tap into the gift of Marital Intimacy [/seasidetms_text][seasidetms_tabs shortcode_id="pxitq4bi3" mode="tab" active="1" animation_delay="0" classes="tabstyle"][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="p2mpw7pj5" title="Course Topics"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="723v3dggp4" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="c10455dxfd" title="Class 1:  Soul Connections"] Insights of Chazal on the holiness and centrality of Mitzvas Onah; the physical-emotional intimacy connection; a boost in our priorities and focus [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="f4ec9ggtgc" title="Class 2:  The Feminine Role In Intimacy."] Fitting intimacy in to the picture of a Jewish woman’s daily life; lessons from our Imahos; a proactive, realistic approach [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="hcd9hq8aam" title="Class 3:  You Were Made For This!"] A frank talk about sexual anatomy and response; the truth about women’s arousal and pleasure; understanding male-female differences in response. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="0hjr89n48" title="Class 4:  Bridging the Gap "] Overcoming different styles, preferences, and needs in intimacy; re-igniting the power of touch. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="jqmaja9j55" title="Class 5:  Embracing Differences"] Boundaries that create more bonding potential; nurturing the “you” and the “me” within the “we” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="101hscyoui" title="Class 6:  When Your Body Says ″No″"] Addressing common obstacles to intimacy: hormones, pain, low desire; practical concerns, practical tools [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="egej47jghu" title="Class 7:  On Again, Off Again:  Tools For Transition"] Dealing with transitions in your intimate life: Niddah, mikvah night, and the art of shifting gears and getting “in the mood” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="ijmodmhsjo" title="Class 8:  From ″Dialogue″ to Heart to Heart"] Creating pathways to discuss intimate issues and resolve conflict; communication techniques that enhance emotional and physical intimacy [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="0g3b0ie1l" title="Class 9:  Creating A Culture of Healthy Intimacy"] Safety and respect in intimacy; positive body image; teaching children healthy sexual attitudes [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="222al0b9gp" title="Class 10:  Tending to the Spark - A Nurtured Marriage"] Keeping intimacy alive and healthy during the ups and downs of married life; nurturing the “relational space” so marriage can thrive Conclusion and takeaways [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="l9x8isr7ll" title="Speaker Profile"]

Hi, I’m Miriam Davidson—wife, mother of six ka”h, individual & couples therapist and intimacy educator.

Born and raised in Boston, MA, I moved to Eretz Yisrael in 2005. I received my degree from the PATH Center for Counseling and Psychotherapy in Israel, with specialty certification as a sex counselor and educator. I’m an IFS practitioner certified by the Israeli Institute for IFS (Internal Family Systems). I worked as an intern therapist at Milam (Family Counseling Center, a division of the Israeli Health Ministry) and I am also a certified JME Kallah Teacher.

In my work I’ve witnessed the central role marital intimacy plays in every couple's life—and how knowledge, tools and support can make all the difference. It’s my honor to help you tap into your innate capacity for growth, healing, and authentic connection.

I feel blessed to live and grow with my family in Yerushalayim.

I can be reached at miriamdavidson18@gmail.com

[/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="sfjnlacu9i" title="FAQ"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="qpewu71kgj" mode="accordion" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="usa5fxaicb" title="What is the format of the classes?"] This is a 14-week teleconference course comprised of 10 classes + two Q&A’s during the course + two additional Q&A’s after the conclusion of the course. The 10 live classes take place on Wednesdays at 10 a.m. Eastern Standard Time (New York) / 5 p.m. Israel time. The course starts November 9, 2022 and ends February 1, 2023, followed by two Q&A’s. This course is structured with two built-in breaks allowing for catch-up, processing, and questions.

The live classes are taught over teleconference. Dial-in access numbers will be provided for whichever country you’re in.

[/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="h2koft1bjf" title="What is the cost of the course?"] The cost of the course is $320. An early-bird special of $305 is offered to those who sign up by October 30. Payment is due in full upon registration. If you’d like to pay in installments or use a different payment method please call 845-659-4795 or email ohelmiriam@gmail.com. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="bhttzt5gpb" title="I can’t join live at that time. Will there be recordings?"] Yes, you can hear the recorded classes any time.

Please note:

For those with email access who wish to receive a weekly recording to your email inbox, please register using this link: https://www.subscribepage.com/simply-together-winter-2022 or email simplytogethercourse@gmail.com

For those without email access who prefer to access the weekly recordings by phone, please register here with Ohel Miriam, either by clicking the “Get Course” button on the right-hand side of this page or by calling 845-400-9559 and pressing 3.

(As mentioned, all participants will join the live classes by dial-in teleconference. The only difference in the two registration methods is in how you want to access recordings.)

[/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="j60hqk1lql" title="s there any visual material accompanying the course?"] Yes. At the start of the course you’ll receive a booklet containing the course schedule, class outlines, diagrams and other visual material. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="p4j4nzwc7" title="Can I join even though the course is already in progress?"] Sure, you can join the course at any time and go at your own pace. I encourage you to pace yourself and listen to one class per week, since each one is packed with info and it’s helpful to give yourself time to process it. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="7a8p5a3y1a" title="Do you have rabbinical backing?"]

I have the support and endorsement of HaRav Binyomin Moskovits, Shlita (Rosh Yeshiva, Yeshivas Midrash Shmuel in Shaarei Chessed, Yerushalayim). Halachic / hashkafic shailos in my work are brought to Rabbi Dovid Broch, Shlita (Shaarei Chessed), and Rabbi Dovid Ostroff, Shlita (Har Nof).

In addition, my husband is a chosson teacher who trained under Rabbi Yitzchak Silver from Yeshivas Mir Yerushalayim, so I refer to his material as well.

[/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="y9iqglubwc" title="What ages and stages is this course for?"] Women married anywhere from one year onward have benefited from this course. One grateful participant recently said: “We’ve been married over 40 years but we’re currently in shanah rishona due to this course!” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="jt908kqc28" title="Can I contact you with questions?"] I welcome your feedback and questions, and I feel that questions are an important part of the learning process. At any time during the course you can email or text me your questions. Twice during the course (following classes 4 and 8) I’ll compile answers to the questions that have come in till then, and you’ll receive a recorded Q&A-only class providing answers to those questions. As a bonus, each week you’ll receive a recording of some of the most common Q&A’s that listeners like you have at that point in the course. At the end of the course, we’ll have two live Q&A classes addressing any questions that remain. If after the completion of the course you feel you have unanswered questions, you may book a private appointment with me over Zoom. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="b9imssnzgw" title="How is the content different from what I learned as a kallah?"]

This course is geared for the married woman who is dealing with the realities of married life. Even in the best of marriages, sexual intimacy needs constant reassessment and chizuk as you and your husband move through different stages.

If you are struggling, this course may help you understand practical or emotional blocks that are keeping you from having a healthy and satisfying intimate relationship—and what you can do about it.

It’s my hope that this course helps you, at whatever stage you find yourself, explore your goals, obstacles and inroads to enhanced intimacy.

[/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="vl2572zwxk" title="I’m a kallah teacher. Will this course be helpful for me?"] I’ve been privileged to teach a number of kallah teachers in my courses, and while this course is not an official training, they’ve shared with me how much it impacted them personally and enriched their work. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="d6ci5jpbb" title="My friend took one of your previous courses. How is this one different?"] Over time I’ve expanded the course to address many of the topics and FAQ’s more in-depth. It started as a 5-part course and as of November 2021 is now 10 parts! So while most of the subjects were covered in earlier courses, we spend more time on them and the classes are structured a bit differently. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="5p8nrlzxhk" title="I took your course and would love to join again! Is there any special rate for second-timers?"] Sure, I welcome past participants to the current course. You can join at the reduced rate of $200. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="cp2ztgw3f4" title="My husband suffers from addiction / mental illness. Is this course for me?"]

This course may be helpful to you if he is receiving treatment. If you and/ or he are in therapy, I suggest you consult with your therapist about whether this course could help your particular situation.

If you and/ or he are not receiving treatment, I don’t recommend you taking this course. Please see how you can address the problem together or seek professional support for yourself in the meantime.

[/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="4d0dhpjdne" title="There’s abuse in my marriage. Can your course help?"] If there’s any kind of spousal abuse (emotional/ verbal/ physical) occurring in your marriage, you’re not alone, but do not sign up for this course. Instead, please seek professional support. You can call Shalom Taskforce at 1-800-799-SAFE(7233) or text “START” to 88788. In Israel, call Tahel at *2511. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="f2i02i4ue" title="I’m a survivor of sexual trauma. Will this course be helpful for me?"]

Due to the sensitive content of our classes, anyone may find them triggering, particularly if you have any kind of trauma in your background.

Depending on where you are in your healing journey, this course may be helpful to you. If you are currently in therapy, please consult with your therapist and decide accordingly.

If you’ve never addressed your background, I encourage you to find a way to do so as a first step before learning about intimacy in this context.

[/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="sxlgc2jeua" title="Testimonial"] [seasidetms_quotes shortcode_id="ikpoairtg" mode="slider" speed="5" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="eylp08mkuq"]
My husband and I agree that this course was worth every penny!
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="yehci6ix4m"]
Since your course ended, I've been re-listening to the classes to review and process everything. Every class is packed with info! Each time I listen, I learn even more.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="5njip10gn0"]
B"H our marriage is great and I wasn't sure I needed this course. But after the first couple of classes I realized how life-changing this information is! I seriously think this course should be required for every married woman.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="b18fn6m9n"]
Thank you for teaching with so much respect for each person and her individual process. I can honestly say that taking your course changed my intimate life.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="qitmpx8cd"]
I've been married 14 years but I feel like I'm in shanah rishona since I started these classes. :)
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="grghnc91l"]
We did the exercise you explained last week, and it was amazing how much it opened things up for us. We learned so much about each other that we hadn't known before.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="84jgfyk87"]
This course has given me such a toolbox of practical information. 
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="rmcbfcvpxr"]
You took my B"H great marriage and have made it spectacular!
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="uhzaztvvz9"]
Intimacy has gone from being a pressure to a pleasure. I’m so grateful to Hashem for sending your course my way.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="lxzr55wdsq"]
Miriam creates a warm safe environment and makes you feel so NORMAL! She speaks from the heart making it so easy to connect to her. Miriam is the real deal.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="f0un0ae5lg"]
I’ve been married 24 years and learned so much I never knew before! I listened to each class twice over and couldn’t get enough.
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="e335vamra"]
Somehow you strike a balance of teaching the most explicit topics with so much openness and clarity but in such an aidel, natural, tzniusdik way. Thank you
[/seasidetms_quote][/seasidetms_quotes] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="4v4jygplt" title="Overview"] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="werhkg6o2w" title="More info"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="403c943qv" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="ji6mdrc0g8" title="Sound Familiar?"] “Intimacy was great when we were newlyweds, but now, with the chaos of daily living, something’s gotten lost.” “It feels like my husband and I are on different planets when it comes to this stuff. Plus, whenever one of us is in the mood, the other is too tired.” “I just don’t enjoy intimacy at all, no matter what we try. I basically do it for my husband. Could it be that something’s wrong with me?” “Ever since I had my baby, intercourse is totally different than it was before.” “I can’t seem to keep distracting thoughts out of my head during intimacy. Help!” “It takes me forever to get aroused. How can I speed things up a little?” “Can someone please tell me I’m normal?” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="kayel2ocs" title="You’re not alone!"] Sexual intimacy is complex and deeply personal. Desires and needs fluctuate throughout married life, and the husband-wife relationship is constantly evolving. So intimacy is—understandably—an area we’re constantly grappling with. Maybe... …You’re having trouble getting aroused and/or feeling pleasure during intimacy. …You feel there is information you’re missing, or questions you have. …You learned about intimacy as a kallah, but now as a married woman—familiar with the strengths and struggles unique to your marriage—you are trying to put it into practice. …You and your husband have such different needs when it comes to intimacy, and this is a constant source of tension. …Your kids are growing up and moving out, and with less people at home, you and your husband are working on rekindling the spark between you. …You feel you need a booster to put intimacy back into its rightful position at the center of your marriage—the beating heart of your home. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="x1ax0i1va" title="Finding the balance"] Jewish intimacy is a topic to be handled with immense respect: with openness and clarity in a context of modesty and sensitivity. But it’s tricky. On one hand this is a delicate topic not to be discussed freely with anyone and everyone. On the other there is often so much misunderstanding and misinformation, and there are so many unanswered questions. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="8xkid49jzv" title="The good news?"] This course is designed specifically with this fine balance in mind. It’s a safe, judgment-free space to explore intimacy from a hashkafic, physiological, and psychological lens, getting the clarity and personal tools you need. Receive sensitive guidance from a professional who gets you, your lifestyle, and your dreams for loving connection. Join women from all over the world who have made real changes and are experiencing a deeper level of joy and closeness in their marriage. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="hu68290hyw" title="In this course you will"]
  • Gain a deeper understanding of the Torah hashkafah on intimacy (based on Chazal as brought in Sefer Ohel Rochel)
  • Absorb a healthy, positive outlook on intimacy
  • Get clarity about sexual functioning, arousal and pleasure
  • Understand "his and hers" differences in intimacy—and learn tools to bridge the gap
  • Explore practical ways to create emotional intimacy in marriage
  • Learn tools to “get in the mood” and start enjoying intimacy more
  • Apply the class material to your personal life with journal exercises and tools
  • Have a chance to get your questions answered
All in a warm, sensitive, professional environment. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="aa7dxjo71k" title="Who is this course for?"] Growth-minded, frum, married women at any stage who are seeking clarity and tools to help them strengthen their connection with their husband (and themselves!) and make marital intimacy more fulfilling. [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][/seasidetms_tabs][seasidetms_css shortcode_id="ikb0t69t4"][/seasidetms_css][seasidetms_js shortcode_id="jioc7pp4d"][/seasidetms_js][/seasidetms_column][/seasidetms_row]

Revitalizing Intimacy Formula

The Complete Program to Create a Passionate Jewish Marriage and Soul Satisfying Oneness.. [/seasidetms_text][seasidetms_tabs shortcode_id="iysfhubqae" mode="tab" active="1" animation_delay="0" classes="tabstyle"][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="odhogu2ewp" title="Course Topics"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="h94z4d1kt" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="wty7ifr6qb" title=" Module 1: Kedusha, Desire and Pleasure-Getting Our Heads On Straight"] You will achieve mind blowing clarity about  intimacy  (the Mitzva of Onah) and its centrality to a Torah home.  With the writings of contemporary and classic sources, many based on Kabbala,you will finally feel a sense of freedom to explore the delights that marriage has to offer in a whole new way [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="gc6nyyp84" title="Module 2: The Female Pleasure System-Connecting to YOU!"] In this module, we discuss the female body,  and the art and science behind experiencing pleasure. Here you will be given  tips,tools and techniques to maximize each intimate encounter.  You will reclaim your amazing feminine  and sensual essence. This class has been an absolute favorite with all of my students, even the ones who are satisfied in their intimate lives! [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="ohkcztkh" title="Am I too young to start?"] If you are married and this is an area of interest for you GO FOR IT!  I wish I would have had this type of course when I was your age.  It doesn't mean you have to get out there and teach today if you don't feel comfortable but it does mean you have begun the first practical step to create your new reality. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="y2ilbl35nf" title="How long do I receive the recordings for?"] Lifetime access. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="bujrzivuba" title="I have done many Kallah Teaching courses, what do I stand to gain by doing yours?"] Good on you for continuing your education so you make a better Kallah Teacher for the Kallos you teach!  This course is not just content but context.  Not only what to teach but how to teach.  Approaching Kallah Teaching from a holistic perspective, understanding the psychological, emotional and physical principles of marriage and intimacy. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="myt3c1avv4" title="I′ve never done a course in my life, will I be out of my depth?"] All of the concepts I teach are clearly laid out and broken up so beginners can hop aboard.  There is question time every class for you to clarify anything you are not clear about and most importantly, questions are welcomed, I've never met a silly question!!! [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="5quthu43zv" title="Can we contact you outside of class hours?"] Yes, send me an email and I'll be more than happy to respond (though it might take a day or so).  Sometimes a Kallah Teacher needs support outside of the course and I am happy to provide up to 15 minutes telephone time as necessary. [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="w2nf7n4is7" title="Testimonial"] [seasidetms_quotes shortcode_id="d0jpnnjjmn" mode="slider" speed="5" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="ewtbfiii8p" name="Rivky Eisenstein, Lakewood, aspiring Therapist and Kallah Teacher"] "I absolutely loved the kallah teaching course and especially Mindy's way of teaching it and putting it all together. The course delivers its purpose with the vast knowledge the Mindy shares. It's enlightening and challenging, given over softly and eloquently. " [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="no3e5srxoq" name="Chaya Wieder, trained Kallah Teacher, trained Marriage & Intimacy Life Coach"] Mindy Wiesner’s Kallah Teaching course is comprehensive, detailed & very practical.  Mindy is a great teacher and shares her many years of experience.  Her course is a practical step by step taking everything you’ve learned from different places and bringing it all together with a great guide and approach to give you the confidence to take on a Kallah! I know many experienced Kallah teachers who also enjoyed the course a lot! Wishing you hatzlucha! [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="qmvl2k6fe5" name="S. Klein - Monsey N.Y."] Mindy's comprehensive course is the secret to success in kalla teaching. I gained a wealth of knowledge and insights and I feel empowered to give over the information to others! [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="7q271hv4or" name="Aidy Weiner, Experienced Kallah teacher, Certified Life Coach"] Mindy has impressive knowledge in an array of topics that are relevant in teaching today's kallah. The halachos are presented and reviewed in a clear and organized fashion. Hashkafic and emotional/psychological issues are explored, thereby vastly enriching the curriculum. This course is truly about an holistic approach to kallah teaching.I highly recommend it to aspiring as well as experienced kallah teachers. [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="x99l5zrmr8" name="S.W. - Kallah Teacher, Monsey NY"] I can’t say enough about this course. Mindy has an amazing breadth of knowledge in all areas connected to טהרת המשפחה and she shares a wealth of information in השקפה, science and psychology. Her non-judgemental approach to people and circumstances is something incredible to learn from, as a Kallah teacher and as a person. She happily shares with us the mistakes she made along the way because they are great lessons. “Living in Unity” is not only a clear and excellent Kallah teaching course, but also an opportunity for a renewed and deeper connection with HaShem and with the world! [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="izv7d4kri8" name="Anonymous"] Mindy's class was an awesome experience. I'm only 23 nowhere close to becoming a kallah teacher but this class enhanced my life in every area. It wasn't just halachus, the class provided a lot of healing through feeling and emotional growth. I think every person no matter what age and stage can benefit from this class immensely. [/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="izv7d4kri8" name="Chaya Chani Rothschild - London"] Mindy's kalla teacher  training course is a holistic program incorporating halacha, upto date hadracha and different therapy modalities coaching the trainees to become healthy professional kalla teachers! [/seasidetms_quote][/seasidetms_quotes] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="ib8jqk7784" title="Overview"] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="6suq6h1gna" title="More info"] [/seasidetms_tab][/seasidetms_tabs][seasidetms_css shortcode_id="l68vdzmqj"][/seasidetms_css][seasidetms_js shortcode_id="k4uwrimy4n"][/seasidetms_js][/seasidetms_column][/seasidetms_row]

[seasidetms_row data_shortcode_id="bd8qdbkxm" data_padding_bottom_mobile_v="0" data_padding_top_mobile_v="0" data_padding_bottom_mobile_h="0" data_padding_top_mobile_h="0" data_padding_bottom_tablet="0" data_padding_top_tablet="0" data_padding_bottom_laptop="0" data_padding_top_laptop="0" data_padding_bottom_large="0" data_padding_top_large="0" data_padding_bottom="50" data_padding_top="0" data_bg_parallax_ratio="0.5" data_bg_size="cover" data_bg_attachment="scroll" data_bg_repeat="no-repeat" data_bg_position="top center" data_color="default" data_bot_style="default" data_top_style="default" data_padding_right="3" data_padding_left="3" data_width="boxed"][seasidetms_column data_width="1/1" data_shortcode_id="d0j41xth49" data_animation_delay="0" data_border_style="default" data_bg_size="cover" data_bg_attachment="scroll" data_bg_repeat="no-repeat" data_bg_position="top center"][seasidetms_text shortcode_id="4f975dkfng" animation_delay="0"]

Miriam Davidson-Thriving Together

Nourishing marital intimacy while you build your family [/seasidetms_text][seasidetms_tabs shortcode_id="pxitq4bi3" mode="tab" active="1" animation_delay="0" classes="tabstyle"][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="00tnjd7bku" title="Course Topics"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="4k54h1jf4u" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="enodv5139j" title="Class 1:  Soul Connections"] Insights of Chazal on the holiness and centrality of Mitzvas Onah; the physical-emotional intimacy connection; a boost in our priorities and focus [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="f8lhu6gqae" title="Class 2:  The Feminine Role In Intimacy."] Fitting intimacy in to the picture of a Jewish woman’s daily life; lessons from our Imahos; a proactive, realistic approach [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="pgyfz7akqe" title="Class 3:  You Were Made For This!"] A frank talk about sexual anatomy and response; the truth about women’s arousal and pleasure; understanding male-female differences in response. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="chd52qgocn" title="Class 4:  Bridging the Gap "] Overcoming different styles, preferences, and needs in intimacy; re-igniting the power of touch. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="vofdmw6uza" title="Class 5:  Differences & Diffrentiation"] Boundaries that create more bonding potential; nurturing the “you” and the “me” within the “we” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="v5ax77p0pb" title="Class 6:  When Your Body Says "No!" "] Addressing common obstacles to intimacy: hormones, pain, low desire; practical concerns, practical tools [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="tto608nf1l" title="Class 7:  On Again, Off Again:  Tools For Transition"] Dealing with transitions in your intimate life: Niddah, mikvah night, and the art of shifting gears and getting “in the mood” [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="shvu3enwuo" title="Class 8:  From "Dialogue" to Heart to Heart"] Creating pathways to discuss intimate issues and resolve conflict; communication techniques that enhance emotional and physical intimacy [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="tto608nf1l" title="Class 9:  Creating A Culture of Healthy Intimacy"] Safety and respect in intimacy; positive body image; teaching children healthy sexual attitudes [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="shvu3enwuo" title="Class 10:  Tending to the Spark - A Nurtured Marriage"] Keeping intimacy alive and healthy during the ups and downs of married life; nurturing the “relational space” so marriage can thrive Conclusion and takeaways [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="ukpw5e9rz" title="Speaker Profile"]
Hi I’m Miriam Davidson—wife, mother of six ka”h, certified intimacy counselor and educator.
Born in Boston, MA, I moved to Eretz Yisrael in 2005. After five years of giving marital education to kallos and married women I saw the need among frum women for a more comprehensive understanding of intimacy and sexuality.
I studied at the PATH Center for Counseling and Psychotherapy in Israel, with specialty training in sex therapy. I worked as an intern therapist at Milam (a division of the Israel Health Ministry) and I am also a certified JME Kallah Teacher.
It’s my honor to accompany you as you find new possibilities for authentic connection in your life and marriage. I help you make intimacy a healthy, nourishing place for both partners.
I feel blessed to live and grow with my family in Eretz Yisrael.
[/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="4c158tk6b" title="FAQ"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="ym4v2yj2sz" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="zs5dmuy5vr" title="What method of payment can I use? Can I pay in installments?"] You can pay by credit card, in one complete payment or up to two monthly payments of ????  each. Registration and payment can be done online at ?????? or by calling 845-422-7956; press 2 for courses; 4 for Miriam Davidson; 3 to join the course.  Leave a message with pertinent details. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="vfo7xgfhl" title="What’s the format of the classes?"] There are six live classes via teleconference. You will receive slides by email before each class??. You are also entitled to one private Q&A session by phone or Zoom after the completion of the course. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="y8xq7tko28" title="Are the classes recorded?"] Each class is recorded in case you miss a class or want to review. You will receive the access number for the recordings upon registration. There is also an option to join the course via recordings if the timing doesn’t work out for you. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="iexxnz4wx9" title="Do you have rabbinical backing?"] Yes. I have the Haskama of HaRav Binyomin Moskovits, Shlita (Rosh Yeshiva, Yeshivas Midrash Shmuel in Shaarei Chessed). Halachic / hashkafic shailos in my work are brought to Rabbi Dovid Broch, Shlita (Shaarei Chessed), and Rabbi Dovid Ostroff, Shlita (Har Nof). In addition, my husband is a chosson teacher who studied under Rabbi Yitzchak Silver from Yeshivas Mir in Yerushalayim, so I refer to his material as well. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="3gesx20599" title="How is the content different from what I learned as a kallah?"] This course is geared for the married woman who is dealing with the realities of married life. Even in the best of marriages, this topic needs constant reassessment and chizuk as you and your husband move through different stages. As a kallah you may not have had the opportunity to learn about intimacy in depth—for example, about your physical and emotional functioning when it comes to intimacy. And if you are struggling, this course may help you understand practical or emotional blocks that are keeping you from having a healthy and satisfying intimate relationship. Each week you’ll receive a journal assignment designed to help you explore your goals, obstacles and inroads to enhanced intimacy in your marriage today. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="d2jtfxmnu" title="Can I contact you privately for questions during the course? "] I welcome your feedback and questions, and love connecting with my students! The last 15 minutes of each class are designated for questions or clarification. Your private half-hour session at the end of the course is designated for any personal questions you have. You may email me questions between classes or leave me a message on the Ohel Miriam line by calling 845-422-7956; press 2 for courses, 4 for Miriam Davidson and 1 to leave a message for me. Please allow me 2-3 days to respond. I may choose to address the question in the group (anonymously and with your permission) if I feel it could benefit everyone. Your personal info is handled with respect and confidentiality. You’re also asked to maintain confidentiality for your fellow course members and not disclose their personal info to anyone outside the class, so we can all enjoy a comfortable learning environment. If you have any further questions or concerns, please feel free to email me at miriamdavidson18@gmail.com Looking forward to taking this journey together! Miriam [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="pxitq4bi3" title="Testimonial"] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="pxitq4bi3" title="More info"] [/seasidetms_tab][/seasidetms_tabs][seasidetms_css shortcode_id="ikb0t69t4"][/seasidetms_css][seasidetms_js shortcode_id="jioc7pp4d"][/seasidetms_js][/seasidetms_column][/seasidetms_row]

[seasidetms_row data_shortcode_id="rjp7xub99n"][seasidetms_column data_width="1/1"][seasidetms_text] [/seasidetms_text][/seasidetms_column][/seasidetms_row][seasidetms_row data_shortcode_id="2yt4xhikkw" data_padding_bottom_mobile_v="0" data_padding_top_mobile_v="0" data_padding_bottom_mobile_h="0" data_padding_top_mobile_h="0" data_padding_bottom_tablet="0" data_padding_top_tablet="0" data_padding_bottom_laptop="0" data_padding_top_laptop="0" data_padding_bottom_large="0" data_padding_top_large="0" data_padding_bottom="50" data_padding_top="0" data_bg_parallax_ratio="0.5" data_bg_size="cover" data_bg_attachment="scroll" data_bg_repeat="no-repeat" data_bg_position="top center" data_color="default" data_bot_style="default" data_top_style="default" data_padding_right="3" data_padding_left="3" data_width="boxed"][seasidetms_column data_width="1/1" data_shortcode_id="d0j41xth49" data_animation_delay="0" data_border_style="default" data_bg_size="cover" data_bg_attachment="scroll" data_bg_repeat="no-repeat" data_bg_position="top center"][seasidetms_text shortcode_id="8mhwyqu8w6" animation_delay="0"]

The Essence of Intimacy (based on the 9 Middos) FOR MEN ONLY

Explore the inner meaning of marital unity from a true Jewish perspective

[/seasidetms_text][seasidetms_tabs shortcode_id="l7l6b1jalg" mode="tab" active="1" animation_delay="0" classes="tabstyle"][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="lvqyzl0ak" title="Course Topics"] The Rabbis delineate 9 circumstances where intimacy is problematic. Chazal refer to them as the “The Tisha Middos”. These negative situations are: fear, force, hatred, strife, temurah, intoxication, intentions of divorce, confusion, and brazenness. Paradoxically, from these 9 negative situations, we can learn the positives- and come to an understanding of the true nature of intimacy, in a way you may have never considered before. [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="twfpijzeq" title="Speaker Profile"] Rabbi Aryeh Nivin is the founder and director of R’ Aryeh Nivin’s Chaburas and the research institute Machon HaAdam Hashalem. He takes Classic Torah Concepts and translates them into practical and powerful tools to improve wellbeing and vitality for his Talmidim and community. Every week he reaches hundreds of individuals throughout the world, in both his private practice and hosting his 5-10 weekly teleconferences. With his down-to-earth manner, warmth, and deep insights, Rabbi Nivin has spent 30 years transforming thousands of lives, helping individuals to live a life of Simcha and Menuchas Hanefesh (serenity). [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="s2qvlb40v" title="FAQ"] [seasidetms_toggles shortcode_id="thbeyhgfa8" mode="toggle" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="ixloj2sk1" title="Is this a comprehensive course on Marital Intimacy?"]  This is series 2 of a comprehensive program that explores Marital Intimacy. Many more topics will be covered in future series [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="sobafmth0g" title="Are there previous Shiurim I can listen to?"] You can listen to the first series, which covers the topic of Piyus, “Hashkafas HaTorah al Simchos Ishto”, for free. This series can be accessed by dialing 845-414-8014.  This line is by caller ID only.  To register for the free series, please email ohelmiriam@gmail.com with your name and phone number.  Registration is for MEN only. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="hmc9zo3in" title="Are the Shiurim downloadable?"] Due to the deeply sensitive nature of the material, these Shiurim will not be downloadable. However, participants will be able to access recordings by phone or computer. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="6ybaa4e9kg" title=" What is the concept of “The 9 Middos”?"] The 9 Middos are nine different situations under which intimacy between husband and wife is problematic. By exploring the negatives, we’ll learn to do the positives- and discover what the essence of Jewish intimacy is. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="akmbvju4yr" title="Will we cover all of the 9 Middos in this course?"] We certainly plan to in a general way, but if participants feel there is a strong need, we might do a series 2 on this topic. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="nptzezz3gn" title=" What is the level of modesty that will be adhered to in this series?"] Due to the extremely delicate and sensitive nature of this material, these topics will be discussed in a general and not overtly explicit manner. However if you feel you need time to discuss more complicated issues, there is an option for private coaching.  Please be aware and understand the complexity of teaching a class like this in a public forum. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="9f2e6hnt7h" title="Does it matter what background I come from?"] The course is taught on a fairly high level, so it’s not recommended for brand new beginners to Jewish thought. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="8stt6jovgq" title="But I took classes back when I was engaged! What will I gain from doing this course?"] This lecture is not about Taharas Hamishpacha. It covers a broad scope of topics within marital intimacy and is much more comprehensive than initial Chosson classes. It is unfortunate that most men haven’t taken any courses on the subject since their engagement days- this course is a rare opportunity to do so. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="kol1pssq5" title="Can women listen to this course?"] No, this course is not suitable for women, and it is a strong infraction of Jewish law for any woman to listen to it for any reason. [/seasidetms_toggle][seasidetms_toggle shortcode_id="1i0z8x1ie9" title="Will I be able to contact Rabbi Nivin outside of class hours?"] During the class, there will be the option to be in contact through text or email. If relevant, the answers will be discussed during the lecture. Between classes, short email answers will be provided, depending on time considerations. If you need more time to discuss more complicated issues, there is an option for private coaching. [/seasidetms_toggle][/seasidetms_toggles] [/seasidetms_tab][seasidetms_tab shortcode_id="tl1hsohtop" title="Testimonial"] [seasidetms_quotes shortcode_id="uvbv6p06k7" mode="slider" speed="5" animation_delay="0"][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="os88nni9ae"]
“I listened to the recordings of the first 2 Shiurim on Piyus and they were truly incredible.
Thank you for putting this together and please continue to do so, this way we can all benefit.
Yasher koach and Hatzlacha Raba!”
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="awcpt8g5is"]
“I listened to the 3 classes, it was amazing, no words,
please email me info about the small chabura.”
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="axr8zru2kj"]
“I found it enlightening and to be honest, straight out shocking at the "chomer ha'inyan"
(the seriousness of these matters).
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="zuxdobcrir"]
“It was very informative and educating.”
[/seasidetms_quote][seasidetms_quote shortcode_id="4d1kemze1j"]

I set out on a quest to MASTER MY MARRIAGE. I spent 18 years learning everything I could about marriage success, taking extensive training courses, becoming certified as a Mastery coach with the Dina Friedman Academy, and discovering what REALLY works!
It pained me that people were struggling and didn’t have the knowledge of how to change their situation. After testing different ideas in my marriage and in the marriages of my clients, and after identifying the tools that equip women to strengthen their marriages and reduce stress in a quick and powerful way, it has become my mission to make sure everyone learns these little known and very impactful tools and techniques that can turn a marriage from lonely and painful to deeply connected and loving. I am committed to giving women the knowledge, tools and support they need to transform their marriages for real.

Body cream based on hemp oil. – A New Sensory Experience
You leave the bathroom and your body is thirsty for soothing moisture, you take out the My shemen body cream, apply it elegantly and enjoy a new sensory experience. The texture, the moisture and the effect of the hemp oil give the body a new freshness that has not yet been known. And the fragrance? Stays and stays for a long time.
My shemen's velvety body cream contains a wonderful mixture of oils collected from the open nature, plus hemp oil that encourages the regeneration of the skin, relaxes and gives it protection from the sun. The cream is also rich in vitamins and amino acids, which help to slow down the aging of the skin, contains glycerin, vitamin E and lactic acid which gives a boost of moisture to the skin. The result - a body that is smooth, flexible and full of moisture that remains throughout the day.
Ingredients and benefits: Hemp oil: the oil has extraordinary health properties for the skin and helps to calm the skin, the blood flow, the decrease in the pain signal and gives a boost of freshness to the skin.
Rich in moisture and contains amino acids and a complex of vitamins that contribute to a healthy and radiant appearance of the skin, while slowing down the aging processes of the skin.
Glycerin: This is the ideal substance for those with sensitive skin and prevents skin dryness in addition to its softening properties. Glycerin helps in the treatment of skin problems, rashes, cuts, injuries and more.
Vitamin E: helps maintain the appearance of young and healthy skin, and slows down the aging of the skin.  Lactic acid: clarifies and affects the activity of creating elastin in the skin, increases moisture, softness and shine.
What are the virtues of the hemp oil ingredient? Cannabis seed oil blends perfectly with the body cream's unique blend of oils, giving the skin deep moisture that penetrates through multiple skin layers. The cannabis oil component, as well as the other carefully selected wonderful ingredients, combine in a wonderful synergy and highlight the special qualities attributed to cannabis.
What is the effect of a cream based on hemp oil?
My shemen adds hemp oil in the exact dose that creates local relief for a certain area. When hemp oil is absorbed by the skin, it begins to progress to the lower layers of the skin even before reaching the muscle, and is absorbed into the endocannabinoid system. This system exists naturally in everyone, and it strives to maintain balance through signals received from CB1 and CB2 receptors. Amazingly, both CB1 and CB2 receptors are found in the skin.
Is there a restriction on daily use of hemp oil? No. There is no limit to the amount of use of the cream containing hemp oil, so you can use it throughout the day.
Does a cream based on hemp oil help skin problems?
Certainly. One of the unique benefits of hemp oil for treating skin problems is its anti-inflammatory activity, which helps skin problems. In addition, hemp oil acts as an antioxidant that helps remove toxic waste from the skin
A velvety, rich body cream that intensely nourishes and hydrates the skin . Formulated with C annabis O il , rich in vitamins and amino acids , that help s protect against environmental damage and premature skin aging. Hand Cream – Keep your Palms Fresh All Year Round
Harsh humidity, change of seasons, contact with cleaning agents and a variety of other actions we do with our hands, make them look neglected and even dry and cracked.
How do you keep your palms fresh? What ingredients must be in your hand cream? Everything is right here.
For some reason, many women take care of their facial skin, but somehow the hands do not receive the same attention, even though they are exposed to harm and an active and busy part of our body. But as with facial skin care, we must also take care of our hands in order to prevent dryness, aging and conditions of cracks and redness.
So it's true that the dryness starts, you run to the pharmacy and buy a hand cream that contains as much moisture as possible, but have you checked what your cream contains? Most likely not.
It’s important to get a cream that softens the skin long term that gives your hands over time the moisture and nourishment that they really need.
So what is recommended to have in your hand cream?
Always prefer to use a high-quality hand cream containing natural oils combined with vitamins and amino acids. For example, hemp oil, rich in vitamins and amino acids, provides the skin with an anti-aging effect and a soft, velvety texture. Additional ingredients are allantoin, which is a plant substance extracted from comfrey root that fights irritation and inflammation, vitamin E is known for its properties as a strong antioxidant that protects the body's cells, lactic acid that provides natural moisture and regenerates cells, and mainly mixtures of natural oils.
How will you protect your palms over time?
Daily application of a quality hand cream
My Shemen Hand cream is excellent for treating dry skin, because it contains a wonderful mixture of oils plus vitamins, natural acids and other natural substances. Daily application of the cream will leave your hands soft and pleasant throughout the day, slow down skin aging and prevent irritation and dryness!
Clean your hands moderately.
It is true that hand washing is recommended to maintain hygiene, but it is not recommended to exaggerate hand washing beyond what is necessary. If washing is not necessary, it is preferable to avoid soap or wash only with water and wipe well with a towel.
Protect your hands from detergents
There is nothing like washing the house with bleach and feeling the sponge with your hand, but the detergents dry out the hands, so she preferred to use gloves when cleaning the house or when using different cleaning agents. If you don't want to wash your hands every time you wash dishes, apply a hand cream beforehand that will protect your hands from the detergents.
Pamper your palms
Our hands work non-stop - typing, changing diapers, cleaning, packing and what not. To allow the blood to flow to the wrists and allow the hands to rest, apply hand cream and massage them for a few seconds, this way you will help the blood to flow and allow the hands to "breathe". Protect your palms from the sun
It is important to protect from the sun not only the face, but also the palms exposed to the sun. By the way, they are the first to develop spots and pigmentation, so apply sunscreen to them as well and prevent them from being exposed to the sun as much as possible.
Pamper your hands with a boost of hydration and nourishment. This luxurious hand cream is formulated with hemp oil - rich in vitamins and amino acids, that provides skin with an anti-aging effect and a soft, velvety appearance.
Contains Allantoin, Glycerin, Vitamin E and Lactic Acid that help accelerate skin regeneration, improve elasticity and protect against environmental aggressors.
Its unique formula is instantly absorbed without leaving a sticky residue, for skin that feels soft and silky smooth.
How to use:
Massage into the skin until fully absorbed. Apply several times a day as needed. Suitable for everyday use.
Caution:
Please use this product only for the purpose for which it was intended and in accordance with thev directions for use. Do not ingest. Avoid contact with the eyes. Keep out of children’s reach. Do not use this product if you are sensitive to any of the ingredients.
150 gr - 5.3 oz

Body Serum
You must know it... the end of the day has come and your skin is dry again and losing its elasticity and vitality and thirsty for moisture. This happens mainly in the cold winter days when you are wrapped in layers, but also in the summer when the clothes are tight (especially the skinny ones) and you move from an air-conditioned place to the scorching sun outside.
To restore lost moisture, you need a powerful serum that will give your skin a boost of moisture that will stay on your skin throughout the day. So let's see what is a body serum? What is his role? And why is it so addictive?
The field of care and cosmetics is full of products, creams and a variety of preparations that allow us to improve the quality of life and overcome dryness, pressures and tensions and especially the weather damage caused to the skin. However, not every product is always suitable for everyone, so you need to understand the goals of each one and what it is intended for in terms of the effect and the result. Body serum is one of the products that is not intended for everyone, but is considered luxurious, so it is important to know when to use it and for whom it is suitable.
What is body serum and what is its main function? The term 'serum' is actually borrowed from the world of vascular medicine. It is a liquid that is produced from distilled blood that does not contain the components of blood clotting, so it is used in conventional medicine to cure a variety of diseases related to the blood vessels. So why is the name of a serum taken from the cosmetics world? Because the product has wonderful abilities and its ability to help and restore conditions that are considered difficult for the skin, and it is mainly known to us as a serum for the face, but it also exists as a serum for the body.
It is important to understand that in the world of cosmetics there are different types of serums intended for treating the body, face, and hair, and they will usually be products intended to treat thoroughly and deeply compared to other products. This is why the ingredients of a serum will be much more intense, and in the case of a body serum, it will contain several times more moisture than a normal body cream you know.
More concentrated and powerful Serums will usually come in small, compact bottles and the material in them will be very concentrated, that is, there will be a concentration of the materials naturally and without additional additives - but purely a restorative and nourishing substance. Due to the concentration of the ingredients of the serum, the bottle will be small and a few drops are enough to use and achieve the desired effect.
Body serum is intended for skin restoration and increased nutrition, especially for those who suffer from extremely dry skin and sometimes also cracked skin on the palms, elbows and knees where the skin is particularly hard. By applying a few drops, the serum ingredients penetrate the inner layers of the skin, are absorbed at peak speed and provide a quick effect of soft skin that is pleasant to the touch.
A little about the unique serum of My shemen If you also suffer from dry, rough and cracked skin, it's time to get to know My shemen's body serum. The serum has a rich velvety texture and provides a boost of increased moisture to all areas of the body. The serum contains oils that encourage skin regeneration, vitamins and amino acids that help protect against environmental damage and slow skin aging. It also contains glycerin, vitamin E and lactic acid which leave the skin supple, soft and silky smooth. And the fragrance?  Helps maintain younger, healthier looking skin that stays and stays throughout the day.
How to use:
Massage onto clean, dry skin until fully absorbed. Recommended for daily use. To boost a body cream, add 3-7 drops and use as directed.

Description

Vaginal dryness and irritation can be a problem for women at all stages of life and may result from many factors including health conditions, medications, hormonal changes, and stress. Restore® Moisturizing Vaginal Gel relieves dryness, eases related discomfort, and reduces abnormal odor. These symptoms are often your body's way of communicating a pH imbalance. If you're feeling off, try Restore® and find your way back to a natural, healthy, and stable vaginal biome.

Ingredients

Organic Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice, Xanthan Gum, Lactic Acid, Natural Flavor, Potassium Sorbate, Sodium Benzoate

Works the Way You Do®

  • Moisturizes to Reduce Dryness, Irritation & Unusual Odor
  • Water-Based with Organic Aloe.
  • Gynecologist Recommended
  • Bio-Match® pH-Balanced Formula Supports Vaginal Health by Promoting Natural pH Levels, Salt & Lactic Acid
  • No Estrogen, Parabens, Petrochemicals, Glycerin or Artificial Fragrance
  • 15 Applications & 1 Easy-to-Clean Reusable Applicators Per Carton
  • pH Rating: 3.6 - 4.1
  • Osmolality Rating: 250 - 400 mOsmol/kg
  • Made in the U.S.A.

How To Use

  1. Wash hands thoroughly before and after product application.
  2. Remove re-usable applicator from wrapping. Pull back the applicator plunger and screw onto tube. Fill applicator with 4 to 5 grams of Restore® gel.
  3.  To ease first-time insertion, apply a small amount of Restore® gel to vaginal opening while sitting or lying on back with knees bent.
  4. Push in plunger to deposit gel. Restore® is most effective at bedtime. Use Restore® for 3 consecutive nights (4 grams) and then 1-2 times per week afterwards.
  5. Wash the applicator in hot soapy water and allow to air dry. Make sure that the applicator is clean and dry before reusing. Restore® can also be applied by hand.
